name: EZ Pawn Corp
description_short: Family-owned NYC pawn chain since 1996, now 17 locations citywide. Offers collateral loans at 4% per month with immediate cash in hand.
description_long:
EZ Pawn Corp is a New York City-based pawnbroking chain founded in 1996 by David Kaminsky, whose father Martin Kaminsky launched Gem Pawnbrokers in Brooklyn in 1947 after immigrating post-WWII. Now spanning three generations of the Kaminsky family, the business has grown from a single location to one of the fastest-expanding pawn chains in the city, with David's children actively involved in operations including marketing.

The company's core offering is collateral-based pawn lending: customers bring items of value to any of their 17 NYC stores, loan officers appraise the item, and the customer walks out with cash in hand along with a loan agreement. The stated rate is 4% per month. Loan terms run from 1 to 4 months based on the on-site calculator. EZ Pawn also operates a buy/sell retail side, purchasing used goods outright and reselling pre-owned merchandise in-store. In compliance with the Military Lending Act, active-duty servicemembers and their dependents are offered loans capped at 36% APR rather than the standard monthly rate.

What distinguishes EZ Pawn Corp from smaller independent pawn operators is the combination of scale, family legacy, and community presence. With 17 locations spread across New York City's five boroughs, geographic convenience is a genuine asset. The company has received press coverage from CBS 2 New York and The New York Times, and it partners annually with charities including the Kiss Cares Coat Drive and the NYC AIDS Walk. Customer reviews highlight friendly, knowledgeable staff and transparent transactions.

For consumers weighing options, EZ Pawn is best approached as a short-term liquidity tool rather than a credit product. The 4% monthly rate equals a 48% simple annual rate, and the loan calculator explicitly warns that fees are approximations and exclude storage, handling, and late charges — meaning the true cost can be higher. There is no indication of an online application or remote appraisal service; borrowers must visit a physical store. The service is geographically restricted to New York City residents.
pros: Three-generation family business operating continuously since 1996, with roots back to 1947
17 NYC locations offer genuine geographic convenience across the five boroughs
Loan rate disclosed upfront: 4% per month, with an on-site calculator to estimate costs before committing
Military Lending Act compliant — active-duty members and dependents capped at 36% APR
Featured in The New York Times and CBS 2 New York, indicating credible community standing
Annual charitable partnerships (Kiss Cares Coat Drive, NYC AIDS Walk) reflect community investment
Immediate cash disbursement — no waiting period once loan is agreed
cons: 4% monthly rate equals ~48% simple APR — significantly more expensive than credit unions, PALs, or personal loans for qualified borrowers
Additional fees (storage, handling, late fees) are explicitly excluded from the loan calculator, making true cost opaque upfront
No online application or remote appraisal — must physically visit a store, which is inconvenient for those outside NYC
Service is entirely limited to New York City; no national availability
About page states 16 locations while homepage states 17 — minor inconsistency suggests content may not be current
best_for: NYC residents who need fast cash against jewelry, electronics, or other valuables and plan to redeem the item within 1–4 months
Consumers with poor or no credit who cannot qualify for a traditional personal loan or credit card
People looking to sell used jewelry, electronics, or collectibles outright for immediate cash
Active-duty military members in NYC who benefit from the legally capped 36% APR rate
diagnosis: EZ Pawn Corp is best suited for New York City residents who need a small, short-term cash advance against a physical asset they intend to reclaim within a few months and who have limited access to traditional credit. The main caveat is cost: 4% per month plus undisclosed storage and handling fees make this among the more expensive short-term borrowing options available, and the service is geographically restricted to NYC.
services: Immediate cash disbursement against pawned items
Military Lending Act compliant loans (36% APR cap for active duty and dependents)
Loan cost calculator available on website
Multi-month loan agreements (up to 4 months per calculator)
